title: Filippo Liviano D’Arcangelo
text:
Research
My research lies at the intersection of historical political economy and economic history. In particular, I study how high culture has shaped the development of modern institutions and influenced patterns of economic growth.
For my master’s thesis, I investigate whether the presence of Enlightenment ideas had a causal impact on support for the French Revolution. To address this question, I adopt a quantitative approach, drawing on a wide range of historical data sources.
Currently working as a Research Assistant for the DRIFT (Deregulation, Reforms, Institutions and Formation of Territorial preference) project directed by Amuitz Garmendia Madariaga
Read more about my research
